steps.title: Read the vertex directly from vertex form
steps.content: The equation $y = -4.7(x - 8.3)^2 + 9{,}800$ is already in vertex form $y = a(x - h)^2 + k$. Read off $(h, k)$ immediately: the vertex is $(8.3,\ 9{,}800)$. No algebra needed.

steps.title: Interpret the vertex in context
steps.content: Since $a = -4.7 \lt 0$, the parabola opens downward, so the vertex is the maximum. The $x$-coordinate $8.3$ is the time in seconds; the $y$-coordinate $9{,}800$ is the height in meters. That means the plane reached a maximum height of $9{,}800$ meters at $8.3$ seconds. That confirms B is correct.
